q: What does 'das Riff' mean in English?
e: Das Riff (noun, neuter) refers to a reef—a ridge of rock, coral, or sand just above or below the surface of the sea. Example: 'Im Riff leben verschiedene Korallenarten und bunte Fische.' (Various coral species and colorful fish live in the reef.)

q: What does 'das Schwein' mean in English?
e: Das Schwein (noun, neuter) refers to a pig—a farm animal with a snout and curly tail. Example: 'Schweine sind Allesfresser.' (Pigs are omnivores.)

q: What does 'verbrauchen' mean in English?
e: Verbrauchen (verb) means to use or consume something. Example: 'Energiesparlampen verbrauchen weniger Strom als herkömmliche Glühbirnen.' (Energy-saving lamps consume less electricity than conventional bulbs.)

s: 'die Verbindlichkeit' means 'obligation, liability, reliability' in German
q: What does 'die Verbindlichkeit' mean in English?
e: Die Verbindlichkeit (noun, feminine) refers to an obligation, liability, or the quality of being reliable. Example: 'Die Ehe ist ein Ausdruck der Verbindlichkeit in einer unverbindlichen Welt.' (Marriage is an expression of commitment in a non-committal world.)

s: 'schleichen' means 'to creep, prowl, sneak' in German
q: What does 'schleichen' mean in English?
e: Schleichen (verb) means to creep or sneak quietly. Example: 'Leise schleichen die Kinder aus dem Bett und zum Kühlschrank.' (The children quietly sneak out of bed and toward the refrigerator.)

q: What does 'unerträglich' mean in English?
e: Unerträglich (adjective) means unbearable or intolerable—something impossible to endure. Example: 'Der Lärm von der Baustelle ist unerträglich.' (The noise from the construction site is unbearable.)

q: What does 'solid' mean in German?
e: Solid (adjective) means solid or sound—referring to something sturdy and well-constructed. Example: 'Eine solide Ausbildung ist die beste Voraussetzung für einen erfolgreichen Berufseinstieg.' (A solid education is the best prerequisite for a successful career start.)

q: What does 'der Spruch' mean in English?
e: Der Spruch (noun, masculine) refers to a saying or slogan—a memorable phrase or statement. Example: 'Die Wände waren mit Sprüchen beschmiert.' (The walls were covered with slogans.)

q: What does 'trocknen' mean in English?
e: Trocknen (verb) means to dry—to remove moisture from something. Example: 'Bei Wind trocknet die Wäsche schneller.' (In the wind, laundry dries faster.)

q: What does 'die Stellungnahme' mean in English?
e: Die Stellungnahme (noun, feminine) refers to a statement or opinion—an official response or position. Example: 'Die Politiker gaben im Anschluss an ihre Unterredung eine Stellungnahme ab.' (The politicians issued a statement following their meeting.)

s: 'die Relation' means 'relation, appropriateness, proportion' in German
q: What does 'die Relation' mean in English?
e: Die Relation (noun, feminine) refers to a relation or proportion—the connection between quantities. Example: 'Der Aufwand steht in keiner Relation zum Ertrag.' (The effort is out of proportion to the return.)

q: What does 'das Profil' mean in English?
e: Das Profil (noun, neuter) refers to a profile—the side view of something or a set of characteristics. Example: 'Winterreifen haben ein starkes Profil.' (Winter tires have a deep tread.)

q: What does 'reflektieren' mean in English?
e: Reflektieren (verb) means to reflect—to bounce back light, sound, or thoughts. Example: 'Weil das Wasser die Sonnenstrahlen reflektiert, bekommt man beim Segeln schnell Sonnenbrand.' (Because water reflects the sun's rays, you get sunburned quickly when sailing.)

q: What does 'die Schlange' mean in English?
e: Die Schlange (noun, feminine) has two meanings: a snake (the animal) or a line/queue (of people waiting). Example: 'Vor dem Museum bildete sich eine lange Schlange.' (A long line formed in front of the museum.)

q: What does 'segeln' mean in English?
e: Segeln (verb) means to sail—to travel on water using sails. Example: 'Am Sonntag will mein Süßer mit mir segeln geh'n.' (This Sunday, my sweetheart wants to go sailing with me.)

q: What does 'vernachlässigen' mean in English?
e: Vernachlässigen (verb) means to neglect—to fail to care for or give proper attention to something. Example: 'Paula arbeitet zuviel und vernachlässigt ihre Tochter.' (Paula works too much and neglects her daughter.)

s: 'unterstellen' means 'to put in charge, imply, store' in German
q: What does 'unterstellen' mean in English?
a: to put in charge, imply, store
e: Unterstellen (verb) can mean to put someone in charge of something, to store something, or to imply something. Example: 'Ihnen wird die Abteilung für Forschung und Entwicklung unterstellt.' (The Research and Development department will be placed under your supervision.)

q: What does 'streben' mean in English?
e: Streben (verb) means to strive or make a great effort toward a goal. Example: 'Birgit strebt nach olympischem Gold.' (Birgit strives for Olympic gold.)

q: What does 'die Selbstständigkeit' mean in English?
e: Die Selbstständigkeit (noun, feminine) refers to independence or self-reliance—the ability to do things on one's own. Example: 'Der Kindergarten will die Selbstständigkeit der Kinder fördern.' (Kindergarten wants to promote children's independence.)

q: What does 'überholen' mean in English?
e: Überholen (verb) means to pass (in traffic) or to overhaul (repair/review). Example: 'Auf der Autobahn darf man nur links überholen.' (On the highway, you can only pass on the left.)

q: What does 'technologisch' mean in English?
e: Technologisch (adjective) means technological—relating to technology. Example: 'Der technologische Fortschritt hat die Kommunikation beschleunigt.' (Technological progress has accelerated communication.)

s: 'die Siedlung' means 'settlement, housing development' in German
q: What does 'die Siedlung' mean in English?
e: Die Siedlung (noun, feminine) refers to a settlement or housing development—a group of buildings in a new area. Example: 'Das Dorf entstand aus einer fränkischen Siedlung.' (The village originated from a Frankish settlement.)

q: What does 'der Strahl' mean in English?
e: Der Strahl (noun, masculine) refers to a ray or beam—a narrow stream of light or energy. Example: 'Die Strahlen der Sonne fallen durch das Blätterdach.' (Rays of sunlight filter through the leaf canopy.)

q: What does 'die Strömung' mean in English?
e: Die Strömung (noun, feminine) refers to a current (in water or air) or a trend. Example: 'Der Fluss hat hier eine starke Strömung.' (The river has a strong current here.)
